Instantiate a variation font.  Run, eg:
$ fonttools varLib.mutator ./NotoSansArabic-VF.ttf wght=140 wdth=85

fontTools.varlib.mutator)	g

}!}"| |!k
_-d%|
t)d't*|
}#|#|
)+an
 Generate a static instance from a variable TTFont and a dictionary
	defining the desired location along the variable font's axes.
	The location values must be specified as user-space coordinates, e.g.:
		{'wght': 400, 'wdth': 100}
	By default, a new TTFont object is returned. If ``inplace`` is True, the
	input varfont is modified and reduced to a static font.
fvarc
axisTagZ
minValueZ
defaultValueZ
maxValue

)+Nr
configLoggerz
fonttools varLib.mutatorz
Instantiate a variable font)
description
inputz	INPUT.ttfz
Input variable TTF file.)
metavar
help
locargsz
AXIS=LOCr
List of space separated locations. A location consist in the name of a variation axis, followed by '=' and a number. E.g.:  wght=700 wdth=80. The default is the location of the base master.)
nargsr]
--outputz
OUTPUT.ttfz7Output instance TTF file (default: INPUT-instance.ttf).)
defaultr]
requiredz
-vz	--verbose
store_truez
Run more verbosely.)
actionr]
--quietz
Turn verbosity off.z
-instance.ttf
DEBUGZ
ERROR
INFO)
level
